title: host_of in tools/web.rs is a hand-rolled URL parser duplicating url::Url
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/tools/web.rs:64-74 (host_of)
## Violation
`host_of` extracts a hostname from a URL by string-splitting on `://`, `/`, and `:`. The crate already depends on `url::Url` (used in `src/web.rs`) which handles scheme detection, IPv6 brackets, percent-encoded hosts, and userinfo correctly. The bespoke parser will misparse e.g. `http://user:pass@example.com/`, IPv6 literals like `https://[::1]:8080/`, and URLs without schemes whose authority is ambiguous.

```rust
fn host_of(url: &str) -> Option<String> {
    let rest = url.split_once("://").map_or(url, |(_, tail)| tail);
    let host_port = rest.split('/').next()?;
    let host = host_port.split(':').next()?.to_ascii_lowercase();
    ...
}
```

Note that the test at line 221 documents the bug: `"://no-scheme"` returns `Some("no-scheme")`, treating a malformed scheme as a host — the function's own tests cement the bug.
## Mandated refactor
Replace with `url::Url::parse(url).ok().and_then(|u| u.host_str().map(|h| h.trim_start_matches("www.").to_ascii_lowercase()))`. Remove the bespoke parser and its broken edge-case tests.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
